title: Medium access control (mac) signaling for reference signal activation and quasi co-location indication in wireless communication networks
abstract: Techniques are provided to control the activation of resource sets via medium access control (MAC) signaling. In some aspect, a radio network node generates a MAC message comprising a first field indicating whether a second field is present, or absent, in the MAC message, the second field identifying one or more resource sets among a plurality of resource sets which are to be activated or deactivated, and a third field comprising quasi co-location (QCL) information. The radio network node then transmits the MAC message to a wireless device.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method in a radio network node, the method comprising: generating a medium access control, MAC, message comprising a first field indicating whether a second field is present, or absent, in the MAC message, the second field identifying one or more resource sets among a plurality of resource sets which are to be activated or deactivated, and a third field comprising quasi co-location, QCL, information; transmitting the generated MAC message to a wireless device. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein when the first field indicates that the second field is present, the third field comprises quasi co-location, QCL, information for each of the one or more resource sets identified in the second field. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the resource sets are measurement resource sets. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the measurement resource sets are channel state information, CSI, measurement resource sets. 5. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the resource sets are reference signal resource sets. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the reference signal resource sets are channel state information, CSI, reference signal resource sets. 7. The method of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the QCL information comprise one or more synchronization signal block, SSB, indices, or one or more transmission configuration indication, TCI, indices. 8. The method of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the first field comprises one or two bits. 9. The method of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the first field consists of one or two bits. 10. The method of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the second field is a bit string comprising a plurality of bits. 11. The method of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the third field is a bit string comprising a plurality of bits. 12. The method of any one of claims 1 to 11, wherein the MAC message is a MAC control element, MAC CE. 13. The method of any one of claims 1 to 12, further comprising determining which one or more resource sets to activate among the plurality of resource sets prior to generating the MAC message. 14. The method of any one of claims 1 to 13, further comprising obtaining the QCL information prior to generating the MAC message. 15. A radio network node adapted to: generate a medium access control, MAC, message comprising a first field indicating whether a second field is present, or absent, in the MAC message, the second field identifying one or more resource sets among a plurality of resource sets which are to be activated or deactivated, and a third field comprising quasi co-location, QCL, information; transmit the generated MAC message to a wireless device. 16. The radio network node of claim 15, wherein when the first field indicates that the second field is present, the third field comprises quasi co-location, QCL, information for each of the one or more resource sets identified in the second field. 17. The radio network node of claim 15 or 16, wherein the resource sets are measurement resource sets. 18. The radio network node of claim 17, wherein the measurement resource sets are channel state information, CSI, measurement resource sets. 19. The radio network node of claim 15 or 16, wherein the resource sets are reference signal resource sets. 20. The radio network node of claim 19, wherein the reference signal resource sets are channel state information, CSI, reference signal resource sets. 21. The radio network node of any one of claims 15 to 20, wherein the QCL information comprise one or more synchronization signal block, SSB, indices, or one or more transmission configuration indication, TCI, indices. 22. The radio network node of any one of claims 15 to 21, wherein the first field comprises one or two bits. 23. The radio network node of any one of claims 15 to 21, wherein the first field consists of one or two bits. 24. The radio network node of any one of claims 15 to 23 wherein the second field is a bit string comprising a plurality of bits. 25. The radio network node of any one of claims 15 to 24, wherein the third field is a bit string comprising a plurality of bits. 26. The radio network node of any one of claims 15 to 25, wherein the MAC message is a MAC control element, MAC CE. 27. The radio network node of any one of claims 15 to 26, further adapted to determine which one or more resource sets to activate among the plurality of resource sets prior to generating the MAC message. 28. The radio network node of any one of claims 15 to 27, further adapted to obtain the QCL information prior to generating the MAC message. 29. A computer program product comprising a non-transitory computer readable storage medium having computer readable program code embodied in the medium, the computer readable program code comprising computer readable program code to operate according to the method of any one of claims 1 to 14. 30. A method in a wireless device, the method comprising: receiving a MAC message from a radio network node; decoding the received MAC message, the MAC message comprising a first field indicating whether a second field is present, or absent, in the MAC message, the second field identifying one or more resource sets among a plurality of resource sets which are to be activated or deactivated, and a third field comprising quasi co-location, QCL, information. 31. The method of claim 30, wherein when the first field indicates that the second field is present, the third field comprises quasi co-location, QCL, information for each of the one or more resource sets identified in the second field. 32. The method of claim 32, wherein the measurement resource sets are channel state information, CSI, measurement resource sets. 34. The wireless device of claim 45, wherein the measurement resource sets are channel state information, CSI, measurement resource sets. 47. The wireless device of claim 43 or 44, wherein the resource sets are reference signal resource sets. 48. The wireless device of any one of claims 43 to 49, wherein the first field consists of one or two bits. 52. The wireless device of any one of claims 43 to 51, wherein the second field is a bit string comprising a plurality of bits. 53. The wireless device of any one of claims 43 to 54, further adapted to, when the first field indicates that the second field is present, activate or deactivate the one or more resource sets identified in the second field. 56. A computer program product comprising a non-transitory computer readable storage medium having computer readable program code embodied in the medium, the computer readable program code comprising computer readable program code to operate according to the method of any one of claims 30 to 42.
